user_story: As an MNO partner, my callback to /api/v1/notification/{type}/{partnerRole}?tenant_key=careerify&channel_key=web-gh-airteltigo reaches the notification service with X-Tenant-Key and X-Channel-Key injected by KrakenD so tenant context is resolved before any work.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 /api/v1/notification/... endpoints in krakend/krakend.json declare input_query_strings containing tenant_key, channel_key (and preserve external-tx-id).
KrakenD injects X-Tenant-Key and X-Channel-Key headers to upstream from those query params using the martian/modifier extra_config block.
Smoke script scripts/smoke/krakend-notification-tenant.sh returns 0; each URL gets a 2xx and notification service logs the resolved tenant_id.

key_facts.current_state: All 6 endpoints have input_query_strings:null and input_headers:null. Backend host is http://127.0.0.1:8082. Each already has headers_to_pass:[external-tx-id].
key_facts.header_injection_mechanism: Use the per-backend extra_config block `modifier/martian` with `header.Modifier` entries. KrakenD substitutes endpoint path/query captures into the value. Example:
key_facts.alternative_header_mechanism_note: If the martian plugin is not in the running KrakenD image, an acceptable fallback is to ONLY set input_query_strings (so query reaches the backend) and rely on the canonical tenantctx resolver in TMP-069 to read tenant_key/channel_key from query. The slice spec explicitly says: 'No services/notification/ code changes expected (existing tenantctx middleware reads X-Tenant-Key/X-Channel-Key headers). Verify; if a small fallback is needed, lift it to TMP-069.' So if martian is unavailable, document the gap and leave the resolver fallback to TMP-069.

scope_out: services/notification/ code changes (no Go code edits unless absolutely required; lift to TMP-069 instead).
risk_boundary: Gateway config change — operator confirmation REQUIRED before commit.
